Scientists say the animal's feathers were chestnut brown on top and pale or carrot-colored underneath.
This discovery also provides deeper insight into the evolution of bird feathers.

The dinosaur feathers did not have a well-developed central shaft—known as a "rachis"—but they still had other feather characteristics, such as barbs and barbules. Researchers infer that the evolution of the rachis may have occurred later than these other features.

The structure of the feathers indicates that they were not used for flight, but rather for temperature regulation or display. Researchers believe that although this is the tail of a young dinosaur, the sample shows mature plumage rather than the feathers of a hatchling.

In France, voting is traditionally a low-tech experience: voters step into a small booth and place a pre-printed slip of paper indicating their chosen candidate into an envelope.

When a small group of organisms (a small population) becomes separated from their original main population (for example, they cross mountains and rivers, or they arrive at a new island so that it is impossible to easily return), they usually find themselves in an environment different from before.

This new environment has different resources and different competitors, so the new population will need different traits or adaptations to become a strong competitor, rather than the ones they needed before.
The original population has not changed at all, and they still need to adapt to the environment as before.
Over time, the new population begins to adapt to the new environment, and they start to become increasingly dissimilar to the other populations.
After thousands or even millions of years, these two populations will eventually look so completely different that they can no longer be considered the same species.
We call this process "speciation", which is the formation of new species. Speciation is an inevitable result of evolution, and also a crucial part of it.

Technological determinism is a term that in practice encompasses many kinds of ideas, including technology push, technological imperative, and a strict definition—that human destiny is driven by the underlying logic associated with scientific laws and its manifestation in technology.

Most interpretations of technological determinism include these two general ideas: first, the development of technology itself largely transcends cultural or political influences, and second, technology in turn has inherent "impacts" on society that are unconstrained by social conditions.
For example, one might say that the automobile inevitably drives the development of roads.
However, building a national highway network for just a few cars is not economically feasible, so new production methods emerged to reduce the cost of car ownership.

Higher car ownership among the general public will also lead to a higher incidence of road traffic accidents, which will drive the invention of brand-new medical technologies for repairing the injuries of the wounded.

Travelers may encounter harmful animals that are not commonly found in their home countries.
Pests can destroy food, cause irritation, and in more severe cases, trigger allergic reactions, transmit venom, or spread infectious diseases.
Infectious diseases themselves, or dangerous animals that can kill humans through physical attacks, are generally not classified as pests.

If you're not used to driving on rural roads, you need to stay alert: there are steep hills, narrow lanes, and sharp curves everywhere.

The posted speed limit here is noticeably lower than the sections before and after it, usually around 35-40 mph (56-64 km/h), and strictly adhering to the speed limit is even more important here than on other stretches of the road.

German pastries are quite good, and Bavarian desserts are even more diverse, much like those of their southern neighbor, Austria.
Fruit pastries are very common. Apples can be used to make desserts all year round, and in summer, you can also enjoy cherries and plums.
Many German baked goods also feature almonds, hazelnuts, and other nuts. Popular cakes are often a perfect match for strong coffee.
If you want a rich-flavored snack, you can try a jam doughnut. This dessert is known by different names in different regions, such as Berliner, Pfannkuchen, and Krapfen.
Curry is a dish primarily based on herbs and spices, accompanied by meat or vegetables.
Curry dishes can be either "dry" or "wet", depending on the amount of water.
In the inland areas of northern India and Pakistan, yogurt is often used in curries, while in southern India and some other coastal areas of the subcontinent, coconut milk is commonly used.
Indonesia has 17,000 islands, so Indonesian cuisine is just a general term that covers a wide variety of regional cuisines across the country.
But without further qualifiers, the term often refers to food originating from the central and eastern regions of the main island of Java.

Now, Javanese food can be found all over the archipelago, featuring many simply seasoned dishes. The main seasonings preferred by the Javanese include peanuts, chili, sugar (especially Javanese coconut sugar), and various spices.
Stirrups are suspended on both sides of the saddle to support the rider's feet.
They provide better stability for the rider, but also pose a safety risk, as the rider's foot might get stuck.
If a rider is thrown from the horse but has one foot caught in the stirrup, they could be dragged as the horse runs. To minimize this risk, certain precautions can be taken.
First, most riders wear heeled riding boots, and the soles are smooth and fairly narrow.
Secondly, some saddles—especially English saddles—are equipped with safety bars, so if a falling rider pulls backward, the stirrup leather will detach from the saddle.
Chile's premier climbing destination, Cochamó Valley, is known as the Yosemite of South America, featuring a wide variety of massive granite walls and cliffs.
The views from the peaks are breathtaking. Climbers from all over the world are constantly pioneering new routes on its endless cliffs, which serve as natural climbing walls.
Alpine skiing, including traditional skiing and snowboarding, is a very popular sport in which skis or a snowboard are attached to your feet to slide down snow-covered terrain.
Skiing is a major tourist activity for many ski enthusiasts, sometimes referred to as "ski bums," who plan their entire vacations around skiing at a specific location.
The idea of skiing has been around for a long time—cave paintings depicting skiers date back to 5000 BC!
As a sport, alpine skiing dates back to at least the 17th century, and in 1861, Norwegians opened the first recreational ski club in Australia.

This activity is related to Alpine ski touring or mountaineering, but usually does not involve these two activities. These two activities are done on steeper terrain and require stiffer skis and boots.
Think of the ski routes as similar to hiking trails.
When conditions are good, you can go further than walking—but only in very rare cases can you achieve the same speed as cross-country skiing on a groomed track without carrying a heavy backpack.

Europe is a relatively small continent but has many independent countries. Under normal circumstances, traveling across multiple countries means having to go through visa applications and passport checks multiple times.
However, in this regard, the Schengen Area is a bit like a single country.
As long as you stay within this area, you can usually cross borders directly without having to go through passport control checkpoints again.
Similarly, with a Schengen visa, you don't need to apply for a visa for each Schengen member state separately, saving you time, money, and hassle.

Handicrafts may be defined as antiques, even though their age is much shorter compared to mass-produced items of the same kind.

However, even in the traditional sense, not all Sámi people were involved in large-scale reindeer herding; they made a living through fishing, hunting, and similar activities, and typically used reindeer as draft animals.
Today, many Sámi people work in modern industries. Tourism is a major source of income in Sápmi, also known as the Sámi region.

Severe weather is a general term for any dangerous weather phenomenon; such weather can cause damage, serious social disruption, or loss of life.
Severe weather can occur anywhere in the world. There are various types of severe weather, depending on geographical location, topography, and atmospheric conditions.
Strong winds, hail, excessive precipitation, wildfires, along with thunderstorms, tornadoes, waterspouts, and cyclones, are all manifestations and impacts of extreme weather.
Regional and seasonal severe weather phenomena include blizzards, snowstorms, ice storms, and dust storms, among others.
Travelers are highly advised to pay attention to the risks of severe weather in their locations, as these may affect travel plans.
Anyone planning to travel to a country that might be considered a war zone should receive professional training.

This course typically covers all the issues discussed here, often delving deeper through practical experience.
The training course usually lasts 2 to 5 days, involving role-playing, first aid training, and sometimes weapons training.
Books and magazines about wilderness survival are common, but publications on survival in war zones are rare.

Governments vary in their willingness to issue passports with an unspecified gender (X), or to amend documents to match a desired name and gender.
Foreign governments' recognition of these documents also varies widely.
In the era after September 11, 2001, searches at security checkpoints have also become more intrusive.
Pre-operative transgender individuals have to expose their privacy when passing through scanners, and their dignity cannot be guaranteed.
A rip current is a strong flow of water rushing back from the beach into the sea, usually occurring around reefs or similar areas.
The underwater terrain causes the return flow to concentrate in a few deeper areas, where rapid currents flowing into deep water can form.
Most deaths are caused by exhaustion from swimming against the current; swimming against the flow is unlikely to succeed.
Once you escape the rapid current, swimming back won't be that difficult.
Swim hard to a place where you won't get trapped again, or wait for rescue (depending on your skills and whether you've been spotted).
Re-entry shock hits faster than culture shock (with a shorter honeymoon phase), lasts longer, and can be more severe.
For travelers who easily adapt to a new culture, it is sometimes difficult to readjust to their native culture.
When you return home after living abroad for a while, you will find that you have adapted to the new culture and lost some habits of your home culture.
When you first go abroad, people can be very patient and understanding, because they know travelers need time to adjust to a new country.
People might not realize that travelers also need patience and understanding on their return journey.

Once the ancient city of Smyrna, it has now become a modern, developed, and busy commercial center, surrounded by a huge bay and mountains.

text: There is a tradition of staying awake somewhere outdoors on Easter night to watch the sunrise.
Of course, this tradition has its Christian theological explanation, but it is likely a pre-Christian ritual celebrating spring and praying for a good harvest.

More traditional churches often hold an Easter Vigil on the Saturday night of the Easter weekend, and the congregation usually pours into the celebration as the midnight bell strikes to celebrate the resurrection of Christ.

Compared to the work culture Westerners are accustomed to, Japanese work culture is more hierarchical and formal.
Suits are the standard business attire, and colleagues address each other by their last names or job titles.
A harmonious workplace atmosphere is crucial, emphasizing team effort rather than praising individual achievements.
Employees must obtain approval from their superiors when making any decisions and are required to obey their instructions unconditionally.
